summary: The main concern should be accurately aligning occlusions because correct bite relations ensure balanced force distribution which prevents structural damage; while important, continuous aspects are less critical compared to proper occlusion when considering durability and function over time. To achieve optimal results with minimal risks associated with potential failures from misaligned bites, meticulous attention needs to focus primarily on establishing ideal occlusive patterns before finalizing any restorations involving artificial teeth or implants. Agent1 concludes by emphasizing the paramount significance of occlusion accuracy despite acknowledging the necessity but lesser priority given to continuities such as gap-free attachments within restoration frameworks designed using well-aligned occlusals. This approach aims at enhancing both immediate success rates post-surgery alongside fostering sustainable health benefits through reduced incidences linked directly back to improperly set up jaw
